required_rebuild_rules: Author a brand-new full scene from the source image and review findings.
Do not patch or incrementally edit the prior scene geometry.
Use the prior scene only as negative evidence about what looked wrong.
Preserve source language and visible formulas exactly.
Rebuild the whole page even when the visible defect looks local.

topology_checklist.source_fact: Upper dashed capture ray connects display and smartphone.
topology_checklist.replica_status: Upper dashed orange-red ray is present.

topology_checklist.source_fact: Lower dashed capture ray connects display and smartphone.
topology_checklist.replica_status: Lower dashed orange-red ray is present.

topology_checklist.source_fact: Fast-time arrow points right above the four subframes.
topology_checklist.replica_status: Right-pointing navy time arrow is present.

topology_checklist.source_fact: First subframe feeds the human eye.
topology_checklist.replica_status: First green diagonal arrow reaches the eye.

topology_checklist.source_fact: Second subframe feeds the human eye.
topology_checklist.replica_status: Second green diagonal arrow reaches the eye.

topology_checklist.source_fact: Third subframe feeds the human eye.
topology_checklist.replica_status: Third green diagonal arrow reaches the eye.

topology_checklist.source_fact: Fourth subframe feeds the human eye.
topology_checklist.replica_status: Fourth green diagonal arrow reaches the eye.

topology_checklist.source_fact: The eye feeds the readable display.
topology_checklist.replica_status: Green horizontal arrow reaches the output display.

topology_checklist.source_fact: The selected second subframe feeds the camera.
topology_checklist.replica_status: Orange-red vertical arrow reaches the camera.

topology_checklist.source_fact: Camera output feeds one sparse fragment.
topology_checklist.replica_status: Orange-red horizontal arrow reaches the fragment.

topology_checklist.source_fact: The sparse fragment reaches OCR failure.

visual_checklist.source_expectation: Two panel headings are clear and balanced.
visual_checklist.replica_status: Both panel headings are present and readable.

visual_checklist.source_expectation: Authorized user, sensitive display, and smartphone labels remain readable.
visual_checklist.replica_status: Authorized, PRIVATE, Smartphone, and camera words break into narrow fragments.

visual_checklist.source_expectation: The user-screen-phone triangle reads as one physical eavesdropping scene.
visual_checklist.replica_status: The simplified editable office scene preserves the intended threat geometry.

visual_checklist.source_expectation: Eye, integration label, readable display, and output label occupy separate zones.
visual_checklist.replica_status: Human-eye and integration text overlap arrows and the readable display text is clipped.

visual_checklist.source_expectation: Four thumbnails, fast-time arrow, and subframe label remain distinct.
visual_checklist.replica_status: The subframe label collides with the lower outcome labels and green routes crowd the row.

visual_checklist.source_expectation: Camera, instantaneous-sampling label, fragment, and OCR failure are cleanly separated.
visual_checklist.replica_status: Instantaneous sampling and fragment labels overlap the object row and each other.

visual_checklist.source_expectation: All labels remain readable at 3.50-inch width.
visual_checklist.replica_status: Several English words are broken or compressed beyond publication quality.

visual_checklist.source_expectation: Human and camera paths remain distinguishable without color alone.
visual_checklist.replica_status: Icons, solid/dashed grammar, selection bracket, and outcome labels provide redundant distinction.

findings_digest.summary: Physical-scene labels break at single-column size
findings_digest.visible_diff: The source keeps Authorized user, PRIVATE DATA, and Smartphone camera intact, while the replica splits several words into narrow fragments.
findings_digest.source_appearance: Large dedicated label zones around the user, monitor, and smartphone.
findings_digest.replica_appearance: Narrow text boxes compete with the editable office geometry.
findings_digest.impact_on_fidelity: The threat scenario cannot be read reliably after reduction.
findings_digest.expected_visible_change: Widen label zones, reduce local type slightly, and enlarge the two display text areas.

findings_digest.visible_diff: Green arrows, Human eye, Temporal integration, Readable, and PRIVATE DATA occupy the same narrow band.
findings_digest.source_appearance: Eye and readable display are dominant objects with labels in separate whitespace.
findings_digest.replica_appearance: Labels overlap arrows and the readable display text clips.
findings_digest.impact_on_fidelity: The perceptual asymmetry is visually ambiguous.
findings_digest.expected_visible_change: Create a dedicated top object row, a separate lower caption row, and wider output display text boxes.

findings_digest.visible_diff: The rapid-subframe caption and instantaneous-sampling labels crowd the frame row and lower camera chain.
findings_digest.source_appearance: Subframe, camera, sampling, fragment, and OCR labels use separate vertical zones.
findings_digest.replica_appearance: Captions overlap adjacent routes and objects.
findings_digest.impact_on_fidelity: The selected-one-frame mechanism is difficult to parse.
findings_digest.expected_visible_change: Reserve a caption band under the frame row and move camera-path labels below their object row.

execution_contract: Use the original image and current replica image as the primary visual evidence.
Use structured review findings to author a brand-new full scene.
Use topology_checklist, visual_checklist, and arrow_plan_repair_targets as explicit regeneration inputs.
For cited arrow ids, repair the mapped scene_edge_ids or motif_bindings rather than relying on broad natural-language arrow notes.
Do not patch or copy prior-scene geometry.
Render the new scene and pass it through the no-op gate before claiming a new round.
